matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
seeded_markers: rs1805010 (IL4R Gln576Arg) (IL4R missense type 2 signaling marker)
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests an IL-4/IL-13 receptor signaling thesis: the individual may carry a research-grade modifier of type 2 cytokine signaling, IgE biology, asthma, atopy, or eczema context. Do not use this alone for biologic therapy selection.

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: GT-confirmed rs1801275 (IL4R Ile50Val) dosage suggests this sample carries the IL4R prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests an IL-4/IL-13 receptor signaling thesis: the individual may carry a research-grade modifier of type 2 cytokine signaling, IgE biology, asthma, atopy, or eczema context. Do not use this alone for biologic therapy selection. This specific marker is curated as IL4R missense atopy/asthma marker. rs1801275 (IL4R Ile50Val) is bundled as a IL4R research marker for allergic disease, asthma, eczema, airway inflammation, or immune-regulatory cohort literature. Treat it as low-penetrance, cohort-aware association context rather than as a diagnostic or treatment-selection allele.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched rs1801275 (IL4R Ile50Val) at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the IL4R prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: May tag regulatory, expression, coding, haplotype, or linked-locus variation around IL4R.

methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests an IL-4/IL-13 receptor signaling thesis: the individual may carry a research-grade modifier of type 2 cytokine signaling, IgE biology, asthma, atopy, or eczema context. Do not use this alone for biologic therapy selection. An observed IL4R variant places this sample in the curated IL4R research context. The bundled IL4R knowledge base is focused on asthma, allergy, atopic dermatitis, airway inflammation, epithelial-barrier biology, and immune-regulatory research. Seeded markers and locus models are intended as research context and should not be promoted to diagnostic, prognostic, or treatment-selection claims without external clinical curation. IL4R missense and regulatory polymorphisms are studied as modifiers of atopy, asthma, IgE biology, and type 2 cytokine signaling.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
research_focus: IL-4/IL-13 receptor and STAT6 signaling research

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a IL4R variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. The variant side of the thesis is: The variant observed in this sample suggests an IL-4/IL-13 receptor signaling thesis: the individual may carry a research-grade modifier of type 2 cytokine signaling, IgE biology, asthma, atopy, or eczema context. Do not use this alone for biologic therapy selection. IL4R methylation should be read as local regulatory context around an allergic-inflammation gene. Promoter-proximal or gene-body beta values may help frame accessibility or expression hypotheses, but they are not validated stand-alone biomarkers for asthma, eczema, allergy, immune activation, or treatment response. The bundled IL4R knowledge base is focused on asthma, allergy, atopic dermatitis, airway inflammation, epithelial-barrier biology, and immune-regulatory research. Seeded markers and locus models are intended as research context and should not be promoted to diagnostic, prognostic, or treatment-selection claims without external clinical curation. IL4R missense and regulatory polymorphisms are studied as modifiers of atopy, asthma, IgE biology, and type 2 cytokine signaling.
